intent.summary: Make the emitted consumer workspace `!ass-ade-dev` fully functional out-of-the-box: correct declared runtime deps, editable/wheel install, CLI entrypoint, and documented verification; align with canonical `!ass-ade` rebuild emitter where drift occurs.
deliverables: `pyproject.toml` declares all imports reachable from `ass_ade.cli` on a clean venv
`python -m venv .venv && pip install -e . && ass-ade --help` succeeds without PYTHONPATH
Document emitter hot paths and re-emit workflow so future regenerations do not drop deps
Optional follow-up: copy or smoke-test subset from canonical `tests/` into emitted dev (not in current emitter)

testsAndCommands: Canonical: `cd C:\!aaaa-nexus\!ass-ade && pytest` (subset: tests touching rebuild if time-boxed)
Consumer OOTB: `cd C:\!aaaa-nexus\!ass-ade-dev && python -m venv .venv && .venv\Scripts\pip install -e . && .venv\Scripts\ass-ade --help`
Wheel: `python -m build` or `hatch build` from `!ass-ade-dev` when build tool present

queries: Hatchling wheel packages multiple top-level packages pyproject.toml
hops.findingSummary: Hatch documents `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]` with explicit `packages = [...]` for non-default layouts; editable installs use wheel target selection for path setup.

verificationEvidence.note: Consumer already lists ass_ade + a0–a4 under hatch wheel packages; matches Hatch docs pattern.

queries: arXiv MCP tool calling agents benchmark 2025 2026
hops.findingSummary: Recent arXiv work (e.g. MCPAgentBench, MCPToolBench++, MCPMark) stresses realistic multi-step MCP tool use and retrieval under large tool catalogs—relevant to why `ass_ade.cli` wires MCP helpers and payload validation early in the import graph.

verificationEvidence.note: Literature claim only; no local benchmark reproduction in this pack.

deliverableScopes.deliverable: Wheel contents for multi top-level dirs

risksAndGaps: Emitted `!ass-ade-dev` can drift from canonical `package_emitter._DEFAULT_PYPROJECT_DEPS` if root `pyproject.toml` is not regenerated after emitter changes (jsonschema was present in vendored emitter but missing from consumer root deps until this session).
No `tests/` directory in `!ass-ade-dev`; CI cannot mirror canonical pytest without emitter/orchestrator extension.
sdist vs wheel: Hatch `packages` is wheel-oriented; sdist file selection may need explicit `include` if publishing to PyPI (see Hatch issues on global vs target tables).
AAAA-Nexus and MCP runtime behavior still require valid keys and network; OOTB here means import/install/CLI only.
